title: Roasted Rich Irish Beetroot Gratin
description: A crowd-pleasing Irish dish featuring roasted beetroot in a creamy, buttery sauce. Fodmap-friendly.
instructions: Wrap each beetroot in foil and roast for 45 minutes, or until tender.
While the beetroot cooks, heat the butter and olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Stir in the lemon juice, thyme, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Remove the beetroot from the oven and let cool slightly.
Unwrap the beetroot and peel off the skin.
Slice the beetroot into 1cm thick rounds.
In a separate saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, or until lightly golden.
Gradually stir in the milk, and cook until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the feta and parmesan cheese until melted and smooth.
Transfer the beetroot slices to a baking dish and spoon the cheese sauce over the top.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden and bubbly.
